---
title: "Minh-Phuong Le"
image: img_Phuong.jpg
about: 
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rounded
  image-width: 15em
  links:
   - icon: envelope-fill
     text: Contact
     href: mailto:Minh-Phuong.Le@univ-paris1.fr
   - icon: linkedin
     href: https://vn.linkedin.com/in/phuong-minh-le-b7042b105
   - icon: google scholar
     href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=8zQOwAkAAAAJ&hl=en
---

![](data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAoAAAASCAYAAABit09LAAAAQUlEQVR42mNgIBdkF5b+B2GiFOFVjK4Iq2JcilAUE1IEV/yfSMAwFABJviakCO5rfIoxggabYpzhh6yYYCBTHBMAs1jacRPuSTQAAAAASUVORK5CYII=){alt=""}

I am an applied economist working on climate migration, labor markets, firm behaviors and policy evaluation in developing countries.

Currently a consultant at the World Bank, I work on several expert assignments in Tunisia and Djibouti, covering migration dynamics, gender inequalities in the labor market, and the targeting of social transfers. I am also an associate researcher at the Joint Research Unit Development and Societies (UMR D&S) and qualified as a Maître de Conférences in Economics (CNU section 05, 2025–2029).

My current research focuses on climate migration and its consequences: how climate shocks shape internal and international migration decisions, and how forced displacement affects host communities. In parallel, I extend my work on labor market wage inequality by examining the impact of digitalization on skill and qualification demand in developing countries, using an original job posting database constructed through web scraping.\
